description_long: This sculpture depicts a muscular, kneeling male figure with a dramatic, contorted posture, his head thrown back and chest thrust forward. The figure is missing both arms and lower legs, and the surface shows signs of weathering and damage, with visible chips and erosion. The torso and upper body are highly detailed, with defined musculature and anatomical accuracy, suggesting classical or Hellenistic influence. The figure is mounted on a rectangular plinth, which appears to be made of the same material as the sculpture itself. The coloration is a pale, off-white with darker patches and stains, likely from age and exposure. The overall impression is one of expressive suffering or struggle, with the dynamic pose capturing a moment of intense emotion.
description: A weathered, kneeling male figure with missing limbs and detailed musculature, mounted on a rectangular plinth. The sculpture has a pale, aged surface with visible damage and stains.
description_view_0: The left side of the kneeling figure is visible, showing the dramatic arch of the back and the head thrown backward, with the left arm and leg both missing.
description_view_1: The front view reveals the muscular torso, the expressive tilt of the head, and the absence of both arms and lower legs, with the chest and abdomen prominently displayed.
description_view_2: The right side highlights the curvature of the back and the right knee, with the figure's posture and missing limbs clearly visible from this angle.
description_view_3: The rear three-quarter view shows the back muscles and the way the figure's body twists, emphasizing the dynamic pose and the attachment to the plinth.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a simple plinth, suggesting an indoor or outdoor public setting such as a museum, gallery, or plaza.
